category_title: Black seventh-grade girls with learning disabilities who use multimodal writing to express identity and critique society
scenario_title: Grappling with vulnerable topics and peer judgment in poetry
minimal_context: Raquel is meeting individually during an ELA poetry residency to write a poem about body image.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's start working on your poem about body image for the poetry residency.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I had told my mom about this, how I felt, and I said I wanna get it out and put it into a poem.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What do you want to put in the poem?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: It’s about, like, how people talk about me, like being skinny and stuff and calling me names.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stops writing and looks down) Yeah, and like—and then I thought about that poem yesterday cuz when I had wore a skirt to school, I was really scared.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's start working on your poem about body image for the poetry residency.

STUDENT: I had told my mom about this, how I felt, and I said I wanna get it out and put it into a poem.

TUTOR: What do you want to put in the poem?

STUDENT: It’s about, like, how people talk about me, like being skinny and stuff and calling me names.

TUTOR: Write that down. What else?

STUDENT: (Stops writing and looks down) Yeah, and like—and then I thought about that poem yesterday cuz when I had wore a skirt to school, I was really scared.

task.content: Writing a poem grappling with the topic of body image
task.learning_goal: Express identity and critique society through multimodal writing

context.environment: ELA class during a month-long poetry residency
context.social_setting: Working with a professional artist and meeting individually

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When grappling with deeply tied identity topics like body image and facing peer judgment or name-calling
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Expresses fear about peer name-calling and hesitation about physical expression, stating she was really scared to wear a skirt
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Really scared, feeling judged about being skinny, grappling with topics deeply tied to her identity
related_quotes: It’s about, like, how people talk about me, like being skinny and stuff and calling me names.
when I had wore a skirt to school, I was really scared.

behavior_patterns.condition: When dealing with personal feelings and societal critique regarding her body
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Tells her mom how she feels and expresses a desire to get it out and put it into a poem
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Desire to express herself, own her body, and claim her identity as a worthy, beautiful young woman
related_quotes: I had told my mom about this, how I felt, and I said I wanna get it out and put it into a poem.
